introduction: Superficial yeast infection of the skin caused by Malassezia furfur species (normal commensal on the skin). Common in warm humid climates. Predisposing factors include occlusion of the skin with pomades and greases, immune suppression, hyperhidrosis, and heat.

features: May be generalized in the immuno-compromised
Fine scaly, guttate or nummular patches, particularly on young adults who perspire freely
Individual patches are dirty, yellowish/ brownish/hypopigmented macules (hence the term versicolor)
Sometimes follicular tendency is marked; more noticeable at the advancing edges of the irregular patches
Sites of predilection: Sternal region, sides of the chest, shoulders, upper back, face

drug: Selenuim sulphide shampoo: Apply on affected areas daily, leave on for 10 – 15 minutes and wash off. Continue for 3 weeks
Miconazole cream for limited areas. Apply twice daily for 3 weeks

complications: None usually; only of cosmetic significance
Malassezia furfur sepsis from contamination of the lipid-containing medium in immunocompromised patients receiving hyperalimentation through tubes
prevention: Avoid hot, humid environments or clothing that promote perspiration
Take a cold shower after perspiration
Use any of the above shampoo washes once a month if predisposed
